title: Update tests for UUID-based bead IDs
description: Update all tests in test_orchestrator.py that hardcode sequential bead IDs or assert on ID format. Tests should work with the new B-xxxxxxxx format. Key areas: (1) Tests that create beads and check IDs match B{digits} pattern, (2) Child ID tests expecting B0001-test format, (3) Tree hierarchy tests with multi-level IDs like B0001-1, B0001-1-1, (4) FakeRunner and any test helpers that reference bead IDs. Add new tests: UUID format validation, prefix resolution (exact, unique prefix, ambiguous, no match), backward compatibility with old sequential IDs, creation-time sorting, collision resistance (two allocations produce different IDs).
acceptance_criteria: All existing tests pass with updated ID expectations
New test for UUID format: B-{8 hex chars}
New test for prefix resolution: exact match, unique prefix, ambiguous error, no match error
New test for backward compatibility: old B0001 IDs load alongside new UUIDs
New test for creation-time sorting in list_beads()
New test verifying two allocate_bead_id() calls produce different IDs
No tests rely on sequential ordering of bead IDs

handoff_summary.completed: Ran full test suite (149 tests, 0 failures). Verified all new tests: test_allocate_bead_id_returns_uuid_format, test_allocate_bead_id_returns_unique_ids, test_allocate_bead_id_via_create_bead_uses_uuid_format, test_resolve_bead_id_exact_match, test_resolve_bead_id_prefix_match, test_resolve_bead_id_no_match_raises, test_resolve_bead_id_ambiguous_raises, test_resolve_bead_id_no_beads_dir_raises, test_list_beads_sorted_by_creation_time, test_old_sequential_ids_coexist_with_uuid_ids. All existing tests updated and passing.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Tests are self-contained and do not depend on external state.
handoff_summary.next_action: No further action required. All tests pass.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
